# [FLAWED] PRD: Community Publication Website

*This PRD was generated by AI from the brief: "Our committee needs a way to publish articles online." It contains realistic flaws for you to find and fix.*

---

## Executive Summary

Build a modern digital publishing platform with content management, user registration, commenting, social sharing, newsletter integration, RSS feeds, analytics, and community features for a neighbourhood community association.

## Problem Statement

The community association communicates through a Facebook group, but not everyone is on Facebook and important content gets buried. They need their own publication site.

## Target Users

| User | Description |
|---|---|
| Reader | Browses articles, leaves comments, shares on social media, subscribes to newsletter, creates an account to save favourite articles |
| Writer | Logs in to the CMS, writes articles in Markdown format, configures SEO metadata for each post, manages their author profile and bio |
| Admin | Manages all content, moderates comments, manages user accounts, views analytics dashboard |

## Scope — What We're Building

### 1. Content Management System
Writers log in and create articles using a Markdown editor with live preview. Configure SEO metadata (title tag, meta description, Open Graph image, canonical URL) for each post. Schedule posts for future publication. Manage article categories and tags.
- Acceptance criteria: Writers can create and publish articles

### 2. Reader Accounts
Readers register with email to save favourite articles, set reading preferences, and receive personalised recommendations.
- Acceptance criteria: Readers can manage their reading experience

### 3. Comments
Readers leave comments on articles. Threaded replies. Upvoting and downvoting. User profiles with comment history.
- Acceptance criteria: Comments foster community discussion

### 4. Social Sharing
Share buttons on every article for Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, and email. Share count display. Social card preview optimisation.
- Acceptance criteria: Articles are easy to share

### 5. Newsletter
Mailchimp integration for weekly digest emails. Automated emails when new articles are published. A/B testing for subject lines. Subscriber segmentation by interests.
- Acceptance criteria: Newsletter grows the audience

### 6. RSS Feed
Full-text RSS feed for all articles. Category-specific feeds. Podcast feed support.
- Acceptance criteria: Content is accessible via RSS readers

### 7. Analytics Dashboard
Page views per article, unique visitors, reading time, bounce rate, referral sources, newsletter conversion rates. Integrated with Google Analytics.
- Acceptance criteria: Analytics inform content strategy

### 8. Search
Full-text search across all articles with filters for date, category, author, and tags. Autocomplete suggestions.
- Acceptance criteria: Readers can find content quickly

### 9. Author Profiles
Each writer has a profile page with bio, photo, social links, and a list of their published articles.
- Acceptance criteria: Authors are credited and discoverable

## Technical Stack

- Frontend: Next.js with server-side rendering for SEO
- Backend: Headless CMS (Strapi) with GraphQL API  
- Database: PostgreSQL
- Search: Algolia for full-text search
- Email: Mailchimp API for newsletters
- CDN: Cloudflare for static assets
- Analytics: Google Analytics + custom dashboard
- Hosting: Vercel for frontend, AWS for backend

## Ticket Breakdown

1. Set up headless CMS and infrastructure
2. Build article creation and publishing with Markdown editor
3. Build reader registration and profiles
4. Build commenting system with threading and voting
5. Implement social sharing and Open Graph tags
6. Integrate Mailchimp newsletter
7. Build RSS feeds
8. Integrate analytics
9. Build search
10. Build author profiles
11. Deploy

---

*Find and fix every flaw. The client brief says: "Our committee needs a way to publish articles online." Four writers, none technical. That's it.*
